title: aceras pyramidale var. brachystachys
intro: Aceras pyramidale var. brachystachys, also known as the green orchis or green orchid, is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the family Orchidaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and diuretic effects, which help in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, providing various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, as well as supporting cardiovascular and immune system health. Aceras pyramidale var. brachystachys can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use requires careful consideration due to its endangered status and potential side effects.
